codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of the Uniswap Trade API integration. All 4 endpoints (check_approval, quote, swap, order) are correctly implemented in UniswapClient with proper x-api-key authentication from VITE_UNISWAP_API_KEY. The high-level swap() function properly chains approval → quote → swap/order. Types are comprehensive and match the Uniswap Trade API schema. 30 tests pass covering all client methods, the swap() orchestration, error paths, and barrel re-exports. Three non-blocking issues noted below.

issues.description: For classic swaps, `transactionHash` is set to `swapResponse.requestId`, which is the API's request tracking ID — not an actual on-chain transaction hash. The SwapResponse returns a transaction object at `.swap` that must be broadcast by the caller's wallet; the real tx hash only exists after broadcast. The field name `transactionHash` in SwapResult is misleading for classic routing. Consider renaming to `identifier` or documenting the distinction.

In early error branches (approval failure at line 100, quote failure at line 121), `quoteResponse` is assigned `null as unknown as QuoteResponse` — a double type assertion that breaks type safety. The SwapResult type should make `quoteResponse` nullable (`QuoteResponse | null`) instead of forcing an unsafe cast. Callers checking `result.quoteResponse` after a failure will get null at runtime despite TypeScript saying it's QuoteResponse.

When `approvalNeeded` is true, the function returns the approval transaction data but proceeds immediately to get a quote and execute the swap. If the token hasn't been approved yet, the swap API call will likely fail. This design is fine as an information-passing pattern (caller broadcasts approval tx, then calls swap() again), but the function's JSDoc doesn't mention this two-call workflow. A brief doc comment or README note would prevent caller confusion.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker skill file at .factory/skills/synthesis-worker/SKILL.md does not exist. The feature's skillName is 'synthesis-worker' but the file is missing. Cannot verify whether the worker followed the skill's procedure, or whether the skill needs updates.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Glob for .factory/skills/synthesis-worker/SKILL.md returned no results. The Skill tool was invoked in the transcript skeleton ('synthesis-worker') but the file is absent from the repo.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly placed library code in src/lib/uniswap/ (matching the existing pattern of src/lib/ for utilities) and tests in src/test/ per AGENTS.md guidance. No convention violations detected.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: New files: src/lib/uniswap/client.ts, types.ts, swap.ts, index.ts, src/test/uniswap-client.test.ts — all follow established patterns.

summary: PASS. The Uniswap Trade API integration is well-implemented with clean architecture: UniswapClient class covers all 4 Trade API endpoints with proper x-api-key auth, the swap() function correctly chains the 3-step workflow (approval → quote → swap/order) with both classic and UniswapX routing paths, and TypeScript types are comprehensive. 30 tests provide good coverage. Three non-blocking issues were found: (1) misleading `transactionHash` field name for classic swaps (it's actually requestId), (2) unsafe `null as unknown as QuoteResponse` type assertions in error branches, and (3) missing documentation about the approval-then-swap two-call pattern. None of these block functionality.
